channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Behind the Scenes with James Caan on the Set of The Godfather (1972)
item.description: James Caan’s experience on the set of The Godfather (1972) was a mix of high-energy improvisation, playful pranks, and intense physical scenes. Originally considered for the role of Michael Corleone, ...

item.title: Pakistanis and Iranians Repatriated from US-Seized Vessels with Singapore and Thailand’s Help
item.description: On Friday, Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar confirmed that 11 Pakistanis and 20 Iranians, who were aboard vessels recently seized by the United States in international waters, have...

item.title: Trump's China Visit Yields Limited Trade Gains but Maintains Diplomatic Stability
item.description: Donald Trump's two-day state visit to China in May 2026 concluded with modest tangible outcomes but notable diplomatic significance. Despite mutual praise exchanged between Trump and Xi Jinping, inclu...

item.title: Jason Biggs and Jenny Mollen End Their 18-Year Marriage Amicably
item.description: After 18 years together, actors Jason Biggs and Jenny Mollen have announced the end of their marriage. The couple released a joint statement confirming the split, emphasizing that they remain on good ...

item.title: Matatu Operators Plan Fare Increase Amid Rising Fuel Costs, Call for Urgent Government Talks
item.description: Matatu operators in Kenya have declared plans to raise fares by up to 50 percent following a sudden spike in fuel prices, warning that a nationwide strike could begin from Monday if the government doe...

item.title: US Cancels Planned Deployment of 4,000 Troops to Poland During European Force Adjustments
item.description: The United States has cancelled the scheduled deployment of 4,000 soldiers to Poland, according to US officials. This decision comes as Washington is reorganising its military presence in Europe follo...

item.title: Bruce Springsteen declines to high-five former Governor Chris Christie at concert
item.description: During a recent Bruce Springsteen concert, the legendary musician notably avoided a high-five with former New Jersey Governor Chris Christie, who is also a former presidential candidate. The incident ...

item.title: Capital Football AGM at Hellenic Club draws tense questions from clubs over governance and finances
item.description: Capital Football's annual general meeting at the Hellenic Club in Canberra turned tense as club representatives voiced strong concerns about the organisation's direction. Around 40 attendees, includin...

item.title: PM Modi clarifies no tax or cess will be imposed on foreign travel
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has firmly rejected media reports suggesting that the Indian government was contemplating a cess, tax, or surcharge on foreign travel. According to the reports, such measu...

item.title: Overview of the Recent Pensions Reform and Its Impact on Retirement

item.title: Rotational Brownouts in Luzon and Visayas Due to Power Shortages
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of households and businesses in Luzon and Visayas experienced rotational brownouts recently as the Philippine power grid struggled to meet rising electricity demand. The National...

item.title: Woman Fatally Stabbed by Former Lover in Kilimani, CCTV Footage Shows
item.description: On Thursday, 14th April 2026, Nairobi police and DCI detectives launched an investigation following the tragic death of a woman in Kilimani. The victim, who worked with Newton Ambayi, was attacked whi...

item.title: CBI apprehends key suspect in 2026 NEET-UG chemistry paper leak
item.description: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has apprehended PV Kulkarni, identified as the main accused in the 2026 NEET-UG paper leak. Kulkarni, a chemistry lecturer from Latur, Maharashtra, was involv...

item.title: GetUp! calls for Australian Press Council reform in new campaign
item.description: Progressive advocacy group GetUp! has rolled out a new campaign aimed at reforming the Australian Press Council. The initiative marks the organisation's first major campaign since a tumultuous 2025. A...

item.title: Kerala SSLC Results Announced by Former Rank Holder Principal Secretary Sharmila Mary Joseph
item.description: In a heartwarming coincidence, Sharmila Mary Joseph, Principal Secretary of the General Education Department in Kerala, announced the SSLC examination results on May 15, 2026. She herself was a topper...

item.title: Canadian Web Series ‘Pioneer Girl’ Brings Lesser-Known Women in History to Light
item.description: The new Canadian web series Pioneer Girl, starring Kat Barrell of Wynonna Earp, offers a spirited and humorous take on Canadian history through the lens of women’s contributions, often overlooked in t...

item.title: Tanzania and Russia Advance Strategic Cooperation in Trade, Energy and Mining
item.description: Tanzania is working closely with Russia to strengthen their bilateral relations to a strategic level. On 9th February 2026, Tanzania’s Minister of Foreign Affairs and East African Cooperation, Ambassa...

item.title: Punjab government allows extended business hours until June 1
item.description: LAHORE: The Punjab government has announced that it is easing restrictions on business operating hours across the province until 1st June. Effective immediately, markets, shopping malls, hotels, resta...

item.title: Canadians and Americans buying less alcohol due to affordability and lifestyle changes
item.description: Recent data from Statistics Canada and industry sources reveal a notable decline in beer and alcohol sales across Canada, mirroring trends in the United States. Retail alcohol sales in Canada fell 3.4...

item.title: Supreme Court Denies Request to Protect Draft Senate Flood Control Report
item.description: The Supreme Court has denied a petition seeking a temporary restraining order (TRO) to prevent the Senate and its Blue Ribbon Committee from modifying or discarding drafts of the report on the flood c...
